name: Altos de Mandubia, Santa Ageda y Larraitz — circular through the Aralar Natural Park
name_original: Altos de Mandubia, Santa Ageda y Larraitz — circular por el Parque Natural de Aralar
region_intro: Road cycling around Beasain offers varied terrain and scenic routes through the Basque Country. The region is characterized by its diverse landscapes, including the Aralar Natural Park and views of the prominent Txindoki peak. Cyclists encounter a mix of challenging climbs and more moderate paths, often featuring well-paved surfaces. The area's natural features, such as the Eztanda River, contribute to a rich cycling experience. Best road cycling routes around Beasain The most popular road cycling route is Altos de Mandubia, Santa Ageda y Larraitz — circular through the Aralar Natural Park, a 41.2 miles (66.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 6 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers a challenging circular experience through the Aralar Natural Park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Larraitz Climb – Abaltzisketa to Amezketa Road loop from Beasain, a moderate 29.5 miles (47.5 km) path. This route features a significant climb and mostly well-paved surfaces. Local road cyclists also love the View of Txindoki from Ordizia – Alregi loop from Beasain, a 19.6 miles (31.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain with views of Txindoki, often completed in about 1 hour 18 minutes. Road cycling around Beasain is defined by the Aralar Natural Park, views of Txindoki, and the Eztanda River. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Beasain are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 1,800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Beasain's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Beasain?
A: Beasain offers a diverse selection of over 18 road cycling routes, catering to various fitness levels. These include 2 easy, 12 moderate, and 4 difficult options, ensuring there's a suitable ride for almost every cyclist.

Q: Are there road cycling routes for beginners or those seeking an easy ride in Beasain?
A: Yes, Beasain has routes suitable for beginners. An easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39553821" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Txindoki from Ordizia – Alregi loop from Beasain&lt;/a&gt;. This 19.6-mile (31.6 km) route leads through varied terrain with views of Txindoki and can typically be completed in about 1 hour 18 minutes.

Q: What are the most challenging road cycling routes around Beasain?
A: For experienced cyclists looking for a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1035218313/altos-de-mandubia-santa-ageda-y-larraitz-circular-through-the-aralar-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Altos de Mandubia, Santa Ageda y Larraitz — circular through the Aralar Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route. This 41.2-mile (66.3 km) trail features significant elevation gain, offering a demanding circular experience through the stunning Aralar Natural Park.

Q: Are there moderate road cycling options in Beasain?
A: Absolutely. Beasain offers several moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20526288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Larraitz Climb – Abaltzisketa to Amezketa Road loop from Beasain&lt;/a&gt;, which is 29.5 miles (47.5 km) and features a significant climb on mostly well-paved surfaces. Another moderate choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32121724" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atagoiti – Udana Pass Ascent loop from Beasain&lt;/a&gt;, a 52 km route with notable elevation.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Beasain's road cycling routes?
A: Beasain's road cycling routes offer varied terrain, from challenging climbs with significant elevation gains to more moderate paths. You'll find mostly well-paved surfaces, but some segments might be unpaved. The landscape includes the beautiful Aralar Natural Park, views of the prominent Txindoki peak, and areas along the Eztanda River.

Q: Can I enjoy scenic views of Txindoki on a road cycling route?
A: Yes, several routes offer picturesque views of Txindoki. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39553821" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Txindoki from Ordizia – Alregi loop from Beasain&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33945989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Txindoki from Ordizia – Araxes River Trail loop from Beasain&lt;/a&gt; are excellent choices for cyclists who wish to admire this prominent peak.

Q: What natural landmarks can I see while road cycling in Beasain?
A: While cycling in Beasain, you can encounter several natural landmarks. Routes often pass through or offer views of the stunning Aralar Natural Park. You might also spot the prominent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1016277" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Txindoki (1,346 m)&lt;/a&gt; peak, or cycle near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3958849" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lareo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2916873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barrendiola Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region around Beasain is rich in history. You might encounter the impressive disused viaduct designed by Alexander Lavalley near the Eztanda River, a significant cultural heritage attraction. Other nearby points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2891638" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirandaola Forge and Park&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/836252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Igartza Monumental Complex&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Beasain?
A: The road cycling routes in Beasain are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 1,800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and challenging climbs.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes in Beasain?
A: Many of Beasain's road cycling routes are designed as loops, offering a convenient circular experience. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1035218313/altos-de-mandubia-santa-ageda-y-larraitz-circular-through-the-aralar-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Altos de Mandubia, Santa Ageda y Larraitz — circular through the Aralar Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20526288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Larraitz Climb – Abaltzisketa to Amezketa Road loop from Beasain&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Beasain?
A: While specific weather data isn't provided, generally, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling in the Basque Country, with milder temperatures and less extreme weather. Summer can also be good, though some days might be warmer, and winter can bring colder temperatures and potential rain.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling routes in Beasain?
A: For families or those looking for a less strenuous ride, the easier routes are generally more suitable. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39553821" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Txindoki from Ordizia – Alregi loop from Beasain&lt;/a&gt;, with its moderate distance and varied terrain, could be a good option for families with some cycling experience.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Beasain?
A: While specific parking locations for each route are not detailed, Beasain, as a town, typically offers public parking facilities. It's advisable to check local parking options in Beasain or near the starting points of your chosen route before your ride.
tips: There is a nice restaurant at the top for a coffee and snack

Alto de Santa Ageda is a mountain pass in the Basque Country and is located near the towns of Lapurzulo and Oleta, with a typical arrival of a mountain pass in the Basque Country, always surrounded by green!

Abalcisqueta (in Basque and officially Abaltzisketa) is a locality and Spanish municipality located in the southern part of Toulouse. Abalcisqueta lies within the Aralar Sierra at the foot of Mount Txindoki (1318m).
